title: PropTech Market Size Expected to Reach USD 165 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Unknown
description: The PropTech market is experiencing rapid expansion, valued at $43 billion in 2025 and projected to reach $165 billion by 2035 with a 13.9% CAGR. Growth is driven by AI-powered property management solutions, smart building technologies, cloud computing, and blockchain-based transactions. The U.S. market alone is expected to grow from $11 billion to $42.3 billion over the same period. Integrated platforms dominate with 68% market share, while cloud deployment and residential properties are expected to show the fastest growth rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key player in the rapidly expanding PropTech market with strong projected growth of 13.9% CAGR through 2035

title: Lumentum Holdings Inc. to Join the Nasdaq-100 Index® Beginning  May 18, 2026
author: Na
description: Nasdaq announced that Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE) will replace CoStar Group, Inc. (CSGP) as a component of the Nasdaq-100 Index effective May 18, 2026. The Nasdaq-100 Index tracks 100 of the largest Nasdaq-listed non-financial companies and is tracked by over 200 investment products with $600 billion in assets under management globally.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 Index is a positive development as it increases visibility, liquidity, and typically attracts passive investment flows from the 200+ investment products tracking the index.
Removal from the Nasdaq-100 Index is generally negative as it results in reduced visibility and potential outflows from passive investment products that track the index.

title: Tech Rallies Ahead Of Nvidia Earnings, Bitcoin Jumps 7%: What's Moving Markets Wednesday?
author: Piero Cingari
description: Markets rallied on Wednesday with risk appetite returning as investors positioned ahead of Nvidia's earnings report. The Nasdaq 100 climbed 1.2%, S&P 500 rose 0.7%, and Bitcoin jumped 7%. Tech and software stocks led gains, while energy and consumer staples lagged. Notable earnings movers included strong performances from Circle Internet Group, CAVA Group, and Everus Construction Group, while GoDaddy, First Solar, and CoStar Group declined.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock up 2.31% ahead of highly anticipated earnings report. Viewed as key catalyst for broader technology sentiment and AI-driven earnings momentum.
Down 25% year-to-date but benefiting from software sector rebound for second consecutive session. Set to report earnings after market close.
Strong earnings report fueled renewed optimism in digital asset infrastructure. Shares rallied 29%.
Jumped 25.05% on positive earnings results.
Advanced 31.58%, one of the top gainers on the day.

title: 3 Large Cap Stocks Announce Big Buyback Boosts Amid +20% Falls
author: Leo Miller
description: Three large-cap stocks—Automatic Data Processing (ADP), CoStar Group (CSGP), and Paychex (PAYX)—have announced significant share buyback programs totaling $8.5 billion combined, despite experiencing substantial stock price declines of 20-32% from their highs. The buyback announcements suggest management confidence that shares are undervalued, though all three companies face headwinds from a weakening job market and increased competitive pressures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fiscal Q1 earnings beat with steady growth and margin expansion forecasted. $6 billion buyback program (5.8% of market cap) signals management confidence despite 20% stock decline. Upcoming Jan. 28 earnings report presents near-term catalyst.

Beat earnings estimates and raised full-year 2025 guidance. $1.5 billion buyback (5.4% of market cap) and increased 2026 guidance with moderating investment spending demonstrate management confidence. Strong competitive positioning in commercial real estate with growth potential in residential market.

Met/exceeded earnings estimates but faced 32% stock decline from highs. $1 billion buyback program (2.6% of market cap) shows management confidence, and company has capacity to increase buyback pace. However, hiring market uncertainties and acquisition-related costs create headwinds.

title: Roblox, First Solar, And League Enterprise Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Jan. 5-Jan. 9): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of January 5-9, 2026. Notable losers include Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (down 11.57%), First Solar (down 14.46% following a Jefferies downgrade), Astera Labs (down 12.18%), and Roblox (down 9.99%). Other major decliners include CoStar Group, American International Group, NRG Energy, Las Vegas Sands, Johnson Controls International, and HDFC Bank. The declines were driven by analyst downgrades, leadership transitions, and executive changes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock lost 11.57% during the week with no specific catalyst mentioned, indicating broader market weakness or company-specific concerns.
Stock declined 14.46% following a Jefferies downgrade from Buy to Hold with price target reduction from $269 to $260.
